summary: Atlas Postgres connection pool tuning for 12k TPS
description: Alice: We need to tune PgBouncer connection pool settings for Atlas production. Current config: 50 max connections, causing contention at peak load. Target: increase to 100 max connections in transaction mode. Work with Frank to update the Kubernetes configmap. Redis cache hit rate should absorb the additional read load.

summary: Phoenix: Migrate RabbitMQ consumers to Kafka
description: Bob: Full migration of Phoenix event consumers from RabbitMQ to Apache Kafka. RabbitMQ hit 80k msg/s ceiling in Q4 load test. Kafka target: 600k msg/s with 3-broker cluster on Kubernetes. Create Kafka topics: atlas-events (24 partitions), orion-predictions (12 partitions), horizon-notifications (6 partitions). Replication factor: 3. KRaft mode (no Zookeeper). Coordinate with Alice on Atlas event schema.

summary: Horizon TypeScript strict mode migration
description: Carol: Migrate all Horizon frontend code to TypeScript strict mode. Remove all `any` types. Add ESLint TypeScript rules to CI pipeline to block merges on type errors. The mixed JavaScript/TypeScript codebase has caused production type errors. This decision was approved by Eve in the Q1 frontend summit. Tyler to assist with the component library migration.

summary: Orion PyTorch model training pipeline on GCP
description: Dave: Set up the Orion ML training pipeline. PyTorch was chosen over TensorFlow (see ADR-004). Training on GCP T4 instances. Orchestrated by Airflow on Kubernetes (Titan). Model artifacts to Google Cloud Storage. Target: 90% prediction accuracy on Atlas production data. Sam is supporting the ML experiments.

summary: Titan Kubernetes migration from AWS EC2 to GCP
description: Frank: Migrate all Meridian services from AWS EC2 to GCP Kubernetes (GKE Autopilot). Terraform to manage all GCP resources. Docker for all containers. Services to migrate: Atlas (FastAPI), Phoenix (Kafka), Orion (FastAPI), Horizon (React/Nginx), Nexus (Nginx gateway). Configure HPA for all services. Set up Prometheus, Grafana, Sentry, Datadog. GCP enterprise agreement reduces costs 30%.

summary: Atlas Postgres schema design for v1.0
description: Alice: Design and implement the Atlas transaction ledger Postgres schema. Tables: transactions (UUID PK, amount, currency, source/dest accounts, status ENUM, JSONB metadata), accounts (UUID PK, owner, type, balance), events (UUID PK, transaction FK, event_type, Kafka offset). Indexes: B-tree on (status, created_at), GIN on JSONB metadata. Grace to review schema before migration from SQLite prototype.

summary: Redis cache layer for Atlas hot reads
description: Frank: Provision Redis cluster on GCP for Atlas hot read caching. Key data to cache: account balances (TTL 30s), transaction status (TTL 5s), feature flags for Horizon (real-time via Redis pub/sub). Target: 85%+ cache hit rate on account balance queries. Carol needs the Redis feature flag store for Horizon's feature flag management UI.

summary: Horizon GraphQL API design and implementation
description: Carol: Design and implement the GraphQL API for Horizon. Chosen over REST-only approach for flexible query patterns. Alice confirmed the Atlas Postgres data model supports GraphQL queries efficiently. FastAPI supports GraphQL via Strawberry. The external API is GraphQL. Internal service APIs remain REST. Target: p99 latency under 100ms. Tyler to implement the TypeScript GraphQL client in Horizon.

summary: Elasticsearch integration for Atlas transaction search
description: Dave: Integrate Elasticsearch for full-text search over Atlas transaction history. MongoDB text search was evaluated and rejected (Elasticsearch gives better relevance). Alice reviewed the index design for the transactions table. The Elasticsearch indices mirror the Postgres transactions table. Connect to Horizon's search UI built by Carol. Resolve the Horizon beta feedback: 'transaction search too slow'.

summary: Phoenix: Kafka consumer groups for Atlas, Orion, Horizon
description: Bob: Configure dedicated Kafka consumer groups for each Atlas, Orion, and Horizon service. Consumer group IDs: atlas-transaction-consumer, orion-training-consumer, orion-inference-consumer, horizon-notification-consumer, prism-analytics-consumer. Lag monitoring for all groups in Grafana. Set up DLQ (Dead Letter Queue) for failed message processing. Backpressure handling required.

summary: Orion FastAPI inference endpoint — latency optimization
description: Dave: The Orion PyTorch inference endpoint needs optimization. Current p99: 95ms. Target: under 60ms. Options: model quantization, smaller PyTorch model, GCP GPU upgrade, batched inference. Sam is running experiments on PyTorch model quantization. The endpoint serves Atlas transaction data to the Horizon dashboard. FastAPI async handlers should help with concurrency.

summary: Prism Kafka Streams real-time analytics layer
description: Bob: Implement the Prism Kafka Streams topology. Consumes atlas-events and orion-predictions Kafka topics. Computes rolling aggregates (1-minute, 1-hour, 1-day windows). Materializes results into Postgres aggregate tables (Alice's schema). Morgan owns the product spec. Carol builds Horizon TypeScript charts (recharts) for Prism data via GraphQL. Q2 GA target.

summary: JWT secret rotation — Q1 2024
description: Frank: Rotate all JWT secrets across Meridian services (Atlas, Phoenix, Orion, Horizon, Nexus). Previous secrets expired March 1. New secrets generated and stored in GitHub Secrets. Alice reviewed the Atlas JWT middleware config. Carol confirmed Horizon JWT client is updated. Next rotation: June 1, 2024. Rachel is tracking this in the security compliance calendar.

summary: Orion: LangChain integration for natural language queries — Q3 SPIKE
description: Dave: Spike on LangChain integration for Orion's natural language query feature. Questions to resolve: 1) OpenAI API vs self-hosted models (TBD — leadership decision needed by Q3). 2) Pinecone as vector store — confirm compatibility with Atlas Postgres data schema. 3) How to integrate with Phoenix Kafka for real-time query results. Sam to prototype. Dave to present findings to leadership by Q3.

summary: Atlas bulk import via Kafka — enterprise customer feature
description: Alice: Build bulk transaction import for enterprise customers. Architecture: customers upload CSV, Phoenix Kafka processes each row through the Atlas transaction ledger. Bob has designed the Kafka consumer pipeline for bulk processing. This addresses customer feedback (7/20 customers requested this). Target: 100k transactions in a single bulk import, processed within 60 seconds via Kafka.

summary: Horizon v2 redesign — TypeScript React implementation
description: Carol: Implement the Horizon v2 redesign (Skyler's Figma designs). Key improvements: Kafka real-time data visualization, Atlas transaction drill-down, Orion prediction panel redesign (addressing customer confusion with confidence scores, working with Dave). All components in TypeScript strict mode. Storybook documentation required for new components. Tyler assists. Q2 launch target confirmed by Eve.

summary: GCP europe-west1 cluster for EU data residency
description: Frank: Set up GCP europe-west1 Kubernetes cluster for EU customer data residency. Atlas Postgres data for EU customers must stay in this region (GDPR requirement, Rachel coordinating). Hiro will assist with the Terraform configuration and the Docker image deployment. The GCP enterprise agreement covers this region. Timeline: TBD — pending Rachel's GDPR compliance review.

summary: Nexus API gateway implementation
description: Frank: Build the Nexus API gateway using Nginx. All external API traffic routes through Nexus first. JWT validation at the gateway level. OAuth integration. Rate limiting per customer account. Routes to: Atlas FastAPI, Orion FastAPI, Horizon GraphQL. Alice defines the Atlas API contract, Carol defines the Horizon GraphQL contract, Dave defines the Orion contract. Target: Q3 launch.

summary: Airflow DAG migration for Orion training pipeline
description: Dave: Migrate Orion PyTorch training pipeline from manual cron jobs to Airflow DAGs. Airflow runs on Kubernetes in the Titan cluster (Frank's team set this up). DAGs needed: daily_model_training (consumes Atlas Postgres data via Phoenix Kafka), model_evaluation (accuracy check), model_deployment (push to GCS, update FastAPI inference endpoint). Sam assists with DAG implementation.

summary: V2 pricing model decision — OPEN
description: Eve: The v2 pricing model needs to be decided before the enterprise sales push (Nathan's team is waiting). Open question: seat-based vs usage-based vs hybrid. Key variable: should Orion ML PyTorch inference calls be metered separately? The GCP inference cost per Orion call is known (Frank has the breakdown). Nathan has enterprise pricing benchmarks. Alice needs to provide Atlas transaction volume projections. Decision owner: Eve. Deadline: end of Q2.
